sourceMovie.title: Heidi
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Heidi (2015) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Jungle Book
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Heidi clicked for you, The Jungle Book (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Journey of Natty Gann
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Heidi clicked for you, The Journey of Natty Gann (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Heidi?
faq.answer: Try The Jungle Book, The Journey of Natty Gann, Gold Diggers: The Secret of Bear Mountain—each shares DNA with Heidi while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Heidi?
faq.answer: Heidi (2015) is best described as Adventure, Family, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Heidi worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
